pages.title: Overview
pages.body:
Five hundred tiny spheres erupt from the origin along randomised directions, spiral outward in the XZ plane, and reset back to the centre when they reach the scene radius. Colour transitions from hot white at the core through orange to deep red at the fringe, mimicking plasma or fire.

Navigate through the pages below to walk through each section of this file.

pages.title: Spawn & Color Helpers
pages.body:
randVel() converts an integer seed into a pseudo-random float in [-1, 1] using a two-frequency sine-cosine hash, giving deterministic but visually uncorrelated velocities without requiring a PRNG state.

fireColor() maps radial distance to a packed RGB integer through three piecewise linear segments: white-to-yellow for the inner 30%, yellow-to-orange for the middle 30%, and orange-to-dark-red for the outer 40%. spawn() normalises the random velocity vector and scales it to a random speed in [1.5, 4.5], then scatters the particle's starting position within a 0.3-unit jitter sphere around the origin.

pages.title: Setup
pages.body: setup() adds a near-black ambient light and a bright orange-red PointLight at the origin, which illuminates the innermost particles most strongly and falls off naturally with distance. All 500 particles share the same low-poly SphereGeometry (subdivision level 4) to keep draw calls fast; each has its own MeshStandardMaterial so colour and emissive can be set individually per frame.

pages.title: Update
pages.body:
Each frame every particle's XZ velocity components are rotated by a small angle (0.6 × dt radians) using a 2D rotation matrix, creating a gradual spiral without requiring trigonometry on the position. The particle is then translated by its velocity scaled by dt. When the radial distance exceeds RESET_DIST, spawn() is called again with a seed perturbed by elapsed time, producing variation in the reborn direction.

Colour and emissive are both set from fireColor(dist) so particles self-illuminate, and scale shrinks from 1.0 at the centre to 0.15 at the edge to reinforce the distance cue.
